children.name: Jens Benedictsen Hollunger I af Bramme

zh.lifeSummary: Henrik Gagge (Assersen)是Asser Gagge的兒子，於大約1255年出生。他至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Henrik Gagge (Assersen)是Asser Gagge的儿子，于大约1255年出生。他至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: Gagge
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Lucien, Marcel, Andre, Fernand, Normand, Donat, Jacques, Rosaire, Adrien, Cecile, Emile.
Altered form (Gagné) of French Gasnier, a cognate of Garnier . Compare Gagner 1, Gagnier 1, Gonia , and Gonyer .
French (Gagné): occupational name for a farmer or peasant, derived from Old French gaaignier ‘to cultivate or work (the land)’. Compare Gagner 3 and Gagnier 2.

variants.name: Gagner
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Cecile, Clovis, Gaston, Gilles, Hilaire, Laurent.
French Canadian: variant of Gagné (see Gagne 1). Compare Gagnier 1.
Swedish: unexplained.

variants.name: Gonia
meanings: Americanized form of French Canadian Gagné (see Gagne 1).
Polish: nickname from a derivative of gonić ‘to chase’.
History: Many of the American bearers of the surname Gonia are descendants of one or another of the brothers Louis and Pierre Gasnier from France (see Gagne ).

variants.name: Gonya
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Mechelle, Patrice.
Americanized form of French Canadian Gagné (see Gagne 1) or its variant Gagnier , found predominantly in New England.
History: Bearers of the surname Gonya are descendants of one or another of the brothers Louis and Pierre Gasnier from France (see Gagne ).

variants.name: Bellavance
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Henri, Napoleon, Alcide, Alphonse, Andre, Fernand, Gabrielle, Germaine, Germine, Josee, Normand.
French Canadian: shortened form of Belleavance, a topographic name from French belle avance ‘beautiful projection, overhang’, apparently related to the site of an estate or property.

History: This surname developed from a secondary surname or dit (‘also called’) name, borne by the French Canadian Louis Gagné, sieur de la Fresnaye and dit Belleavance, a son of Pierre Gagné (originally Gasnier; see Gagne ), who married Louise Picard in Beaupré, QC, in 1673.

variants.name: Gonyer
meanings: Americanized form of French Canadian Gagné (see Gagne 1) or its variant Gagnier , found predominantly in New England. Compare Gonya and Gonyea .
History: Bearers of the surname Gonyer are descendants of one or another of the brothers Louis and Pierre Gasnier from France (see Gagne ).

variants.name: Garnier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Francois, Jacques, Achille, Jean-Pierre, Philippe, Andre, Antoine, Laurent, Marcel, Michel, Olivier, Patrice.

French: from an ancient Germanic personal name, Warinhari, composed of the elements war(in) ‘protection, shelter’ or ‘guard’ + hari, heri ‘army’. This surname, which has an identical etymology to that of English Garner , is also found in Britain. Compare Grenier and Gurnee .

History: Charles Garnier from Tournebu in Calvados, France, married Louise Vézina in QC in 1664. François Garnier from Saint-Cosme-en-Vairais in Sarthe, France, married Jacqueline Freslon in Quebec City, QC, in 1663. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and along with its altered form Gurnee also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Gagnier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Camille, Christophe, Dominique, Pierre.
French Canadian: variant of Gagné (see Gagne 1). Compare Gagner 1 and Gonyer .
In some cases probably also French: occupational name for a farmer or peasant, from an agent derivative of the Old French verb gaaignier ‘to cultivate or work (the land)’. Compare Gagne 2.

nameMeanings.name: Henrik
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: equivalent of Henry ( and Heinrich ), especially common in Denmark.

variants.name: Henry
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

en.lifeSummary: Henrik Gagge (Assersen) was born about 1255 as the son of Asser Gagge. He had at least 1 son.

it.lifeSummary: Henrik Gagge (Assersen) è nato il circa 1255 come figlio di Asser Gagge. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio.

ja.lifeSummary: Henrik Gagge (Assersen)は約1255年にAsser Gaggeの息子として生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Henrik Gagge (Assersen)(은)는 대략 1255년 Asser Gagge의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Henrik Gagge (Assersen) nasceu aproximadamente 1255 filho de Asser Gagge. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Henrik Gagge (Assersen) родился около 1255 г. Его отца звали Asser Gagge. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Henrik Gagge (Assersen) nació aproximadamente 1255 como hijo de Asser Gagge. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo.
